ops.title: Centralize codex invocation via codex-ask executable
ops.body:
# Centralize codex invocation via codex-ask

Since codex 0.15.0 (commit f3c968ea), every codex run goes through `plugins/codex/bin/codex-ask` — Claude Code adds a plugin's `bin/` to the Bash tool's PATH, so it is a bare command wherever the plugin is enabled. The skill and codex-wrapper prose carry **no** shell recipes; a repo-bootstrap invariant test (`test_codex_ask_pins_fast_tier_and_quiet_exec`) fails on any `| codex exec` or `codex exec -c` line reappearing in them.

## Why a script, not prose

Copy-paste recipes with placeholders invite improvisation: an agent filled `S=<your scratchpad directory>` with a repo-relative `.scratch/codex`, and jj auto-snapshot committed the leak into cc-context (2026-07-13) — 13 hours after a prose ban shipped in 0.13.0. Requirements that fail silently or catastrophically when missed now live in one enforced executable:

- `-c service_tier=fast` pinned (without it, xhigh runs 10–30+ min and gets abandoned)
- `-c model=gpt-5.6-sol` default, `-m luna` the only variant; `-e` tunes effort (default xhigh)
- `unset OPENAI_API_KEY` — always ChatGPT-plan OAuth (the ambient key is billing-capped and never mounts hosted image_gen)
- `-c developer_instructions="$(cat plugin AGENTS.md)"` — fails loudly if unreadable (the feed carries the browser rules)
- Absolute scratch paths only: TMPDIR sanitized (macOS mktemp ignores TMPDIR without an explicit template; GNU honors a relative one — both handled), `--scratch` rejects relative paths and paths inside the caller's repo
- Quiet exec: JSONL stream to a log; stdout carries only `REPLY_FILE:`/`LOG_FILE:` plus a failure tail; codex exit code propagates

## Rules

1. Never hand-roll `codex exec` in skills, agents, workflows, or prompts — invoke `codex-ask` (bare, or `"${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/bin/codex-ask"` outside the Bash tool).
2. New knobs belong in the script + the behavioral stub-codex test (`test_codex_ask_scratch_is_non_improvisable`), not in prose recipes.
3. `.scratch/` and `.claude-scratch/` stay gitignored (base template + fleet) as the auto-snapshot backstop.
ops.when: when modifying codex invocation patterns or considering direct `codex exec` calls
